content: Curiosity-driven active learning memory systems (from Topic A) can be dynamically gated by a self-supervised "memorable surprise detector" (from Topic B) that only retains long-term memory traces for environment patterns that violate the agent’s current self-supervised predictive model, rather than retaining all high-curiosity observations. This creates a far more memory-efficient autonomous agent that proactively seeks out new information to learn while avoiding clogging its context window with redundant surprising but ultimately uninformative stimuli.
